learnings.topic: Advanced Analytics and Predictive Insights for Supply Chain
learnings.keyInsight: Predictive analytics maturity model: 5 levels from Descriptive to Autonomous. Decklar data enables Level 4 (Prescriptive) — recommend alternate routes, trigger expedited handling, dynamic SLA adjustment.

40-60% of temperature excursions occur during multi-modal handoffs (truck↔ocean↔air), not in steady-state transit. Decklar store-and-forward + extended battery configurations uniquely address this visibility gap.
73-60-11 Rule: 73% of excursions brief (<2h), 60% occur during first/last mile, 11% catastrophic. Distinguish transient (0-15min) vs sustained (15-120min) vs critical (>120min).

learnings.topic: Honeycomb Event Types and Pharma Compliance Guide
learnings.keyInsight: 9 essential event types for pharma: Temperature Excursion, Shipment Departure, Delivery Confirmation, Geofence Entry/Exit, Device Health Alert, Security/Tamper, Route Deviation, Delivery Risk Prediction, Compliance Documentation.

Four expansion archetypes: Lane Expansion (47%), Sensor Payload Expansion (28%), Integration Expansion (19%), Use Case Expansion (6%). Expansion readiness scorecard identifies 18+ score as expansion-ready. Median time to first expansion: 4.2 months.
8 critical call structures: Discovery, Kickoff, Weekly Check-in, QBR, Crisis, Expansion, Renewal, Offboarding. Each with objectives, prep checklist, and post-call actions.
Multi-sensor correlation (temp + humidity + shock + light) enables detection of 5 failure modes: cascade failures, contamination, theft attempts, dwell abuse, and packaging degradation.

learnings.topic: API Integration & Webhook Best Practices for Enterprise
learnings.keyInsight: Webhook reliability requires immediate acknowledgment (<5s) + async processing. McKesson's 9 event types must be validated by May 21 with signature verification, idempotency handling, and idempotency via event_id deduplication.
Decklar success requires structured 'pipe-cleaning' phase post-launch to optimize geofences, ping frequencies, and waypoint triggers using real data
Five core use case categories (high-value cargo, cold chain, multi-modal, reverse logistics, last-mile) with distinct pain points, success metrics, and expansion opportunities
Decklar differentiates on multi-sensor payload (GPS + humidity + temp) vs competitors doing single-sensor
Voice calibrated: warm precision, emotional transparency, dark humor, unconditional optimism. 'I have a plan. I always have a plan.'

criticalInsights: Battery drain = #1 issue. Prevention: disable interrupts for shipments >14 days
Ocean freight = specialized mode required: 4-hour pings, interrupts disabled, 90+ day projection
Activation failures = light threshold <3%. Prevention: require lamp at activation station
Account config issues = contract limits exceeded. Prevention: build 20% buffer in contracts
First 3 shipments must be meticulously validated — cascade failures preventable
Value quantification: Every customer conversation must include quantifiable ROI — use the '3-30-300' rule for hard savings, soft savings, and strategic value
40-60% handoff risk: Multi-modal transfers are highest risk — ocean freight requires store-and-forward + extended battery
Predictive maturity: Decklar enables Level 4 (Prescriptive) — recommend alternate routes, trigger expedited handling
